transcript.clean_text: bennett punches him out the way he does it a second time bennett relegated to the back of the group giving the win to pascal ackermann welcome to stage 10 of love welter 20. we're at the halfway stage of this 75th edition and today is a relatively straightforward day before a couple of cracking mountain stages on the weekend the final is tough though it should suit the punchers rather than the outright sprinters well once again the peloton made a pretty rapid start clipping along at over 43 kilometers an hour during the first two hours of racing a group of four riders eventually pulled away and the pack were happy to let them eke out a significant advantage the biggest of this welter so far it was up to 11 minutes and 40 seconds with 130 kilometers still to go up at the front then jonathan lastra from kaha rural brent van muer from malotto sudal alexander molinar from burgos bh and pimalictart from total direct energy all well off the pace overall now various teams getting involved in the chase hoping to land a stage win before a busy weekend of gc action to koenig quickstep and mitchelton scott in particular the gap shrinking to around eight minutes with a hundred k remaining and as the day bore on it became clear that the peloton wanted to bring them back but it was still going to be a big ask for sprinters like sam bennett with a real kick up to the finish line in suances and speaking of bennett he was struggling on the day's minor climbs the irishman out for revenge after being relegated in yesterday's sprint but he was fighting tooth and nail just to stay in the peloton mitchelton scott still driving the bunch with dion smith and robert stanard and the gap now under three minutes into the final 30k and game over for bennett who was finally dropped it would soon be game over for the breakaway as well this one heading for a grandstand finish the peloton at closing in with 15k to go although jonathan lastra wasn't quite ready to sit up and when the spaniard was finally caught a couple of riders tried their look solo willie smith who's been very active on this welter followed by remy cavania and evo oliveira but none of them were able to stay away all together again with 3.5 k to go and the overall favourites were starting to flex their muscles guillermo martin the king of the mountains going for it inside the final kilometer but he was powerless to stop the green rocket that is primos roglic rutglich now kicks looking for the hat-trick of wins primos rockledge looking for another 10 second time bonus and it's gonna be on the line a hat trick rockletch wins the stage well primos roglich making it a hat trick of wins at this welter the defending champion also picking up vital bonus seconds on the finish line the slovenian champion is into double figures for grand tour stage wins and he is looking on ominous form ahead of the weekend great to win the stage and uh definitely also great uh to take the lead back i think for our team doesn't really change much in uh in terms of approaching now the next two days we saw we have a strong team a strong team also in the mountains and yeah we just need to do our best and then we see how it goes roglic taking his tenth win of the season ahead of felix gross sharpener andrea baggioli and alex aranburu dan martin and jasper phillipson inside the top ten but richard karapas lost three seconds and that turned out to be rather significant because with his 10-second time bonus roglic has recovered the red jersey he and karapas locked on the same time 25 seconds clear of dan martin logic also has a commanding lead in the green jersey standings 46 points clear of cara pass there were only a few points on offer today in the mountains classification so guillaume martin still leads there from cepcus and carapace and henrik mass doesn't look in any danger of losing the white jersey for the best young rider tomorrow it's really going to kick off in the gc with four category one climbs to cover including a summit finish on the alto de la farapona so expect a big shake-up overall
